content:
3 min read
Just now

--

If you’ve ever built an ecommerce system, warehouse platform, or shipping integration, you’ve probably faced this problem:
Tracking packages across multiple carriers is more complicated than it should be.
Each carrier — USPS, UPS, FedEx, DHL, and others — has its own API, authentication flow, response format, and limitations. Maintaining all of them becomes expensive and time-consuming.
At the same time, USPS tracking access has become more restrictive in recent years, making it harder for smaller developers and businesses to reliably access tracking data without additional complexity or cost.
So I built a simpler solution.
A multi-carrier Tracking API that provides a single endpoint and a consistent JSON response across all supported carriers.

🚀 What This API Does
This API allows you to track shipments across multiple carriers using just one request.
Supported carriers:

• USPS

• UPS

• FedEx

• DHL

• LaserShip / OnTrac

• GoFor

• Roadie

• UniUni
Instead of integrating multiple APIs, you only need one.

🔗 Get Started on RapidAPI
Before using the API, you’ll need to set up access:
👉 Go to RapidAPI and subscribe to the Basic Plan (Free)
https://rapidapi.com/c2wtechnology/api/trackingpackage
Once subscribed, open the RapidAPI Playground, where you can instantly test requests in your browser without writing any code.
From there, you can send your first tracking request by simply providing a tracking number, like this:
GET https://trackingpackage.p.rapidapi.com/TrackingPackage?trackingNumber=9200190312809701574398
📦 One Simple API Call
After setup, tracking a shipment is as simple as calling one endpoint:
Just replace the tracking number with your own shipment ID, and the API automatically detects the carrier and returns tracking information.

📊 Example Response
The API returns a consistent JSON structure, even when certain fields are empty or null.
{

"Delivered": false,
"Carrier": "USPS",

{

🧠 Why This Matters
Normally, developers need to:

• Integrate multiple carrier APIs

• Handle different authentication methods

• Normalize inconsistent response formats

• Maintain separate tracking logic per carrier
This API removes that complexity by providing:
✔ One API
✔ One authentication method
✔ One response format
✔ Full tracking history

💡 Key Features

• One API for all major carriers

• Automatic carrier detection

• Standardized JSON response

• Full tracking history included

• Simple REST integration

• Works with C#, JavaScript, Python, Java

• Free tier available

💰 Pricing (Competitive Positioning)
This API is designed as a cost-effective alternative to platforms such as:

• AfterShip

• TrackingMore
It is especially useful for:

• Developers

• Startups

• Small ecommerce businesses

• Warehouse management systems

• Logistics applications
The goal is to provide a simpler and more affordable way to access shipment tracking without unnecessary platform complexity.

🛠 Example Use Cases

• Ecommerce order tracking pages

• Shipping dashboards

• Warehouse management systems (WMS)

• ERP integrations

• Marketplace logistics systems

🧾 Final Thoughts
The goal of this API is simple:
Make shipment tracking easier by removing carrier-specific complexity.
Instead of building and maintaining multiple integrations, developers can rely on a single API with a consistent response format.
If you’re building anything related to shipping or logistics, I’d love to hear your feedback.

🔗 Links
